article_title: Tokyo's Mujin Closes $233 Million Series D Led by NTT and Qatar's QIA to Scale Its MujinOS Automation Platform
summary: Clean funding report; all 3 sources verified, every quote verbatim, figures internally consistent, Bloomberg-only specifics correctly omitted. Sole warning is an allowlist-only notice (no factual error), so no corrections record is warranted.

content_preview.title: Tokyo's Mujin Closes $233 Million Series D Led by NTT and Qatar's QIA to Scale Its MujinOS Automation Platform
content_preview.summary: Industrial-robotics software maker Mujin raised $233 million in a Series D led by NTT Group and co-led by Qatar Investment Authority, bringing total funding to $411 million as it eyes a 2030 IPO.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Mujin, a Tokyo-based developer of control software for industrial robots, has closed a $233 million Series D funding round led by Japan's NTT Group, with Qatar Investment Authority as co-lead investor. According to [Mujin](https://mujin-corp.com/resources/news/seriesd-announcement-advancing-industrial-automation-intelligent-robotics), the company raised $133 million in equity and secured $100 million in debt financing from a group of leading Japanese financial institutions. The roun...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured 485-word News piece. Clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis framing. Technical depth appropriate; no editorializing or sensationalism.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk (status 200, no archive fallback). source-0.html.gz (Mujin company press release): confirms $233M Series D first close, $133M equity led by NTT Group followed by QIA as co-lead with Mitsubishi HC Capital Realty and Salesforce Ventures, $100M debt from Japanese financial institutions, $411M total to date, Dec 2 2025 close, and both founder quotes verbatim and correctly attributed — Ross Diankov ('...the architecture that will power the next era of industrial automation') and Issei Takino ('Building on the world-class industrial automation technologies we have developed over the past 14 years...'). source-1.html.gz (DC Velocity): confirms $233M / $133M VC / $100M debt, NTT lead, QIA co-lead, $411M total, the MujinOS application list (Order Fulfillment, Palletizing, Bin Picking, Machine Tending, Truck Unloading) verbatim, and the 'significantly broaden its global presence' regional-engineering expansion. source-2.html.gz (Crypto Briefing): confirms 2030 IPO plan, University of Tokyo 2011 spinout, Dec 2 2025 first-tranche close, and MujinOS as a 'no-code platform designed for factory and warehouse automation.' Every claim maps to a cited source.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Financial breakdown internally consistent and fully sourced: $133M equity + $100M debt = $233M Series D; $411M total funding. All names spelled verbatim (Rule 7): Mujin, MujinOS, QIA, NTT, Qatar Investment Authority, Ross Diankov, Issei Takino. CRITICAL CHECK — the Bloomberg-only specifics (>$1B valuation, $3B NY-listing threshold, named customers Toyota and Fast Retailing/Uniqlo) are genuinely ABSENT from all three snapshots AND from the article body/summary/title; no claim rests on the bot-blocked Bloomberg source. The single occurrence of 'valuation' is in 'What We Don't Know', correctly noting the announcement does NOT disclose a post-money valuation. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Analysis section contextualizes (software-first vs. humanoid-hardware narrative, sovereign-wealth appetite) without overstating; no loaded language, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Mujin funding coverage in the archive (only unrelated US robotics-policy article mentions the word). Not a duplicate.
recommendations: Consider adding dcvelocity.com (established supply-chain/logistics trade publication) to config/source_allowlist.txt for future robotics/automation coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, neutral, well-sourced funding report with no factual issues. The lone automated finding is an allowlist-only warning (mujin-corp.com primary source, dcvelocity.com trade press, cryptobriefing.com), not a factual error — consistent with prior reviews, an allowlist-only warning does not require a corrections record. Verdict overridden from the script's default APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to APPROVE. Ready for publication.
